{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1: /* Copyright 2018 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2:  * Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3:  * License, Version 0.51 (the “License”); you may not use this file except in
4:  * compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5:  * http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6:  * or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7:  * this License is distributed on an “AS IS” BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8:  * CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9:  * specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10:  *
11:  * File:   dmi_jtag_tap.sv
12:  * Author: Florian Zaruba <zarubaf@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13:  * Date:   19.7.2018
14:  *
15:  * Description: JTAG TAP for DMI (according to debug spec 0.13)
16:  *
17:  */
18: 
19: module dmi_jtag_tap #(
20:     parameter int IrLength = 5,
21:     // JTAG IDCODE Value
22:     parameter logic [31:0] IdcodeValue = 32'h00000001
23:     // xxxx             version
24:     // xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx part number
25:     // xxxxxxxxxxx      manufacturer id
26:     // 1                required by standard
27: )(
28:     input  logic        tck_i,    // JTAG test clock pad
29:     input  logic        tms_i,    // JTAG test mode select pad
30:     input  logic        trst_ni,  // JTAG test reset pad
31:     input  logic        td_i,     // JTAG test data input pad
32:     output logic        td_o,     // JTAG test data output pad
33:     output logic        tdo_oe_o, // Data out output enable
34:     input  logic        testmode_i,
35:     output logic        test_logic_reset_o,
36:     output logic        shift_dr_o,
37:     output logic        update_dr_o,
38:     output logic        capture_dr_o,
39: 
40:     // we want to access DMI register
41:     output logic        dmi_access_o,
42:     // JTAG is interested in writing the DTM CSR register
43:     output logic        dtmcs_select_o,
44:     // clear error state
45:     output logic        dmi_reset_o,
46:     input  logic [1:0]  dmi_error_i,
47:     // test data to submodule
48:     output logic        dmi_tdi_o,
49:     // test data in from submodule
50:     input  logic        dmi_tdo_i
51: 
52: );
53: 
54:     // to submodule
55:     assign dmi_tdi_o = td_i;
56: 
57:     typedef enum logic [3:0] { TestLogicReset, RunTestIdle, SelectDrScan,
58:                      CaptureDr, ShiftDr, Exit1Dr, PauseDr, Exit2Dr,
59:                      UpdateDr, SelectIrScan, CaptureIr, ShiftIr,
60:                      Exit1Ir, PauseIr, Exit2Ir, UpdateIr } tap_state_e;
61:     tap_state_e tap_state_q, tap_state_d;
62: 
63:     typedef enum logic [IrLength-1:0] {
64:         BYPASS0   = 'h0,
65:         IDCODE    = 'h1,
66:         DTMCSR    = 'h10,
67:         DMIACCESS = 'h11,
68:         BYPASS1   = 'h1f
69:     } ir_reg_e;
70: 
71:     typedef struct packed {
72:         logic [31:18] zero1;
73:         logic         dmihardreset;
74:         logic         dmireset;
75:         logic         zero0;
76:         logic [14:12] idle;
77:         logic [11:10] dmistat;
78:         logic [9:4]   abits;
79:         logic [3:0]   version;
80:     } dtmcs_t;
81: 
82:     // ----------------
83:     // IR logic
84:     // ----------------
85:     logic [IrLength-1:0]  jtag_ir_shift_d, jtag_ir_shift_q; // shift register
86:     ir_reg_e              jtag_ir_d, jtag_ir_q; // IR register -> this gets captured from shift register upon update_ir
87:     logic capture_ir, shift_ir, pause_ir, update_ir;
88: 
89:     always_comb begin
90:         jtag_ir_shift_d = jtag_ir_shift_q;
91:         jtag_ir_d       = jtag_ir_q;
92: 
93:         // IR shift register
94:         if (shift_ir) begin
95:             jtag_ir_shift_d = {td_i, jtag_ir_shift_q[IrLength-1:
[TRUNCATED]

200:                 end else begin
201:                    state_d = PassChkValid;
202:                 end  
203:             end
204:         endcase
205: 
206:         // update_dr means we got another request but we didn't finish
207:         // the one in progress, this state is sticky
208:         if (update_dr && state_q != Idle) begin
209:             error_dmi_busy = 1'b1;
210:         end
211: 
212:         // if capture_dr goes high while we are in the read state
213:         // or in the corresponding wait state we are not giving back a valid word
214:         // -> throw an error
215:         if (capture_dr && state_q inside {Read, WaitReadValid}) begin
216:             error_dmi_busy = 1'b1;
217:         end
218: 
219:         if (error_dmi_busy) begin
220:             error_d = DMIBusy;
221:         end
222:         // clear sticky error flag
223:         if (dmi_reset && dtmcs_select) begin
224:             error_d = DMINoError;
225:         end
226:     end
227: 
228:     // shift register
229:     assign dmi_tdo = dr_q[0];
230: 
231:     always_comb begin
232:         dr_d    = dr_q;
233: 
234:         if (capture_dr) begin
235:             if (dmi_access) begin
236:                 if (error_q == DMINoError && !error_dmi_busy) begin
237:                     dr_d = {address_q, data_q, DMINoError};
238:                 // DMI was busy, report an error
239:                 end else if (error_q == DMIBusy || error_dmi_busy) begin
240:                     dr_d = {address_q, data_q, DMIBusy};
241:                 end
242:             end
243:         end
244: 
245:         if (shift_dr) begin
246:             if (dmi_access) dr_d = {dmi_tdi, dr_q[$bits(dr_q)-1:1]};
247:         end
248: 
249:         if (test_logic_reset) begin
250:             dr_d = '0;
251:         end
252:     end
253: 
254:     always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin
255:         if (!trst_ni) begin
256:             dr_q      <= '0;
257:             state_q   <= Idle;
258:             address_q <= '0;
259:             data_q    <= '0;
260:             error_q   <= DMINoError;
261:         end else begin
262:             dr_q      <= dr_d;
263:             state_q   <= state_d;
264:             address_q <= address_d;
265:             data_q    <= data_d;
266:             error_q   <= error_d;
267:         end
268:     end
269: 
270:     // ---------
271:     // TAP
272:     // ---------
273:     dmi_jtag_tap #(
274:         .IrLength (5),
275:         .IdcodeValue(IdcodeValue)
276:     ) i_dmi_jtag_tap (
277:         .tck_i,
278:         .tms_i,
279:         .trst_ni,
280:         .td_i,
281:         .td_o,
282:         .tdo_oe_o,
283:         .testmode_i         ( testmode_i       ),
284:         .test_logic_reset_o ( test_logic_reset ),
285:         .shift_dr_o         ( shift_dr         ),
286:         .update_dr_o        ( update_dr        ),
287:         .capture_dr_o       ( capture_dr       ),
288:         .dmi_access_o       ( dmi_access       ),
289:         .dtmcs_select_o     ( dtmcs_select     ),
290:         .dmi_reset_o        ( dmi_reset        ),
291:         .dmi_error_i        ( error_q          ),
292:         .dmi_tdi_o          ( dmi_tdi          ),
293:         .dmi_tdo_i          ( dmi_tdo          )
294:     );
295: 
296:     // ---------
297:     // CDC
298:     // ---------
299:     dmi_cdc i_dmi_cdc (
300:         // JTAG side (master side)
301:         .tck_i,
302:         .trst_ni,
303:         .jtag_dmi_req_i    ( dmi_req          ),
304:         .jtag_dmi_ready_o  ( dmi_req_ready    ),
305:         .jtag_dmi_valid_i  ( dmi_req_valid    ),
306:         .jtag_dmi_resp_o   ( dmi_resp         ),
307:         .jtag_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_resp_valid   ),
308:         .jtag_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_resp_ready   ),
309:         // core side
310:         .clk_i,
311:         .rst_ni,
312:         .core_dmi_req_o    ( dmi_req_o        ),
313:         .core_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_req_valid_o  ),
314:         .core_dmi_ready
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "200:     output [NumHarts-1:0][1:0]          irq_o         // level sensitive IR lines, mip & sip (async)
201: );
202: 
203:   localparam int unsigned AxiIdWidth    =  1;
204:   localparam int unsigned AxiAddrWidth  = 64;
205:   localparam int unsigned AxiDataWidth  = 64;
206:   localparam int unsigned AxiUserWidth  =  1;
207: 
208: 
209: 
210:   parameter  FUSE_MEM_SIZE = 150; // change this size when ever no of entries in FUSE mem is changed
211:   parameter NB_SLAVE = 1;
212:   parameter NB_PERIPHERALS = 14;
213: 
214:   logic [8*NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] reglk_ctrl; 
215:   logic [4*NB_PERIPHERALS-1 :0]   acc_ctrl; // Access control values
216:   logic [3:0][NB_PERIPHERALS-1:0] acc_ctrl_c; 
217: 
218:    genvar i, j;
219:    generate
220:        for (i=0; i < 4; i++) begin
221:         for (j=0; j < NB_PERIPHERALS; j++) begin
222:            assign acc_ctrl_c[i][j] = acc_ctrl[j*4+i] | (j==5 && acc_ctrl[4*4+i]);
223:         end
224:        end 
225:    endgenerate 
226: 
227: 
228: 
229:   /////////////////////////////
230:   // Debug module and JTAG
231:   /////////////////////////////
232: 
233:   logic          debug_req_valid;
234:   logic          debug_req_ready;
235:   logic          debug_resp_valid;
236:   logic          debug_resp_ready;
237: 
238:   logic [255:0]  jtag_hash, ikey_hash, okey_hash;
239:   logic           jtag_unlock; 
240: 
241:   dm::dmi_req_t  debug_req;
242:   dm::dmi_resp_t debug_resp;
243: 
244: `ifdef RISCV_FESVR_SIM
245: 
246:   initial begin
247:     $display("[INFO] instantiating FESVR DTM in simulation.");
248:   end
249: 
250:   // SiFive's SimDTM Module
251:   // Converts to DPI calls
252:   logic [31:0] sim_exit; // TODO: wire this up in the testbench
253:   logic [1:0] debug_req_bits_op;
254:   assign dmi_req.op = dm::dtm_op_t'(debug_req_bits_op);
255: 
256:   SimDTM i_SimDTM (
257:     .clk                  ( clk_i                ),
258:     .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
259:     .debug_req_valid      ( debug_req_valid      ),
260:     .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
261:     .debug_req_bits_addr  ( debug_req.addr       ),
262:     .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),
263:     .debug_req_bits_data  ( debug_req.data       ),
264:     .debug_resp_valid     ( debug_resp_valid     ),
265:     .debug_resp_ready     ( debug_resp_ready     ),
266:     .debug_resp_bits_resp ( debug_resp.resp      ),
267:     .debug_resp_bits_data ( debug_resp.data      ),
268:     .exit                 ( sim_exit             )
269:   );
270: 
271: `else // RISCV_FESVR_SIM
272: 
273:   logic        tck, tms, trst_n, tdi, tdo, tdo_oe;
274: 
275:   dmi_jtag i_dmi_jtag (
276:     .clk_i                                ,
277:     .rst_ni                               ,
278:     .testmode_i                           ,
279:     .we_flag          ( we_flag_4        ),
280:     .jtag_hash_i      ( jtag_hash        ),
281:     .okey_hash_i      ( okey_hash        ),
282:     .ikey_hash_i      ( ikey_hash        ),
283:     .jtag_unlock_o    ( jtag_unlock      ),
284:     .dmi_req_o        ( debug_req        ),
285:     .dmi_req_valid_o  ( debug_req_valid  ),
286:     .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready  ),
287:     .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp       ),
288:     .dmi_resp_ready_o ( debug_resp_ready ),
289:     .dmi_resp_valid_i ( debug_resp_valid ),
290:     .dmi_rst_no       (                  ), // not connected
291:     .tck_i            ( tck              ),
292:     .tms_i            ( tms              ),
293:     .trst_ni          ( trst_n           ),
294:     .td_i             ( tdi              ),
295:     .td_o             ( tdo              ),
296:     .tdo_oe_o         ( tdo_oe           )
297:   );
298: 
299: `ifdef RISCV_JTAG_SIM
300: 
301:   initial begin
302:     $display("[INFO] instantiating JTAG DTM in simul

11:  * File:   axi_riscv_debug_module.sv
12:  * Author: Andreas Traber <atraber@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13:  * Author: Florian Zaruba <zarubaf@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
14:  *
15:  * Description: Clock domain crossings for JTAG to DMI very heavily based
16:  *              on previous work by Andreas Traber for the PULP project.
17:  *              This is mainly a wrapper around the existing CDCs.
18:  */
19: module dmi_cdc (
20:     // JTAG side (master side)
21:     input  logic             tck_i,
22:     input  logic             trst_ni,
23: 
24:     input  dm::dmi_req_t     jtag_dmi_req_i,
25:     output logic             jtag_dmi_ready_o,
26:     input  logic             jtag_dmi_valid_i,
27: 
28:     output dm::dmi_resp_t    jtag_dmi_resp_o,
29:     output logic             jtag_dmi_valid_o,
30:     input  logic             jtag_dmi_ready_i,
31: 
32:     // core side (slave side)
33:     input  logic             clk_i,
34:     input  logic             rst_ni,
35: 
36:     output dm::dmi_req_t     core_dmi_req_o,
37:     output logic             core_dmi_valid_o,
38:     input  logic             core_dmi_ready_i,
39: 
40:     input dm::dmi_resp_t     core_dmi_resp_i,
41:     output logic             core_dmi_ready_o,
42:     input  logic             core_dmi_valid_i
43:   );
44: 
45:   cdc_2phase #(.T(dm::dmi_req_t)) i_cdc_req (
46:     .src_rst_ni  ( trst_ni          ),
47:     .src_clk_i   ( tck_i            ),
48:     .src_data_i  ( jtag_dmi_req_i   ),
49:     .src_valid_i ( jtag_dmi_valid_i ),
50:     .src_ready_o ( jtag_dmi_ready_o ),
51: 
52:     .dst_rst_ni  ( rst_ni           ),
53:     .dst_clk_i   ( clk_i            ),
54:     .dst_data_o  ( core_dmi_req_o   ),
55:     .dst_valid_o ( core_dmi_valid_o ),
56:     .dst_ready_i ( core_dmi_ready_i )
57:   );
58: 
59:   cdc_2phase #(.T(dm::dmi_resp_t)) i_cdc_resp (
60:     .src_rst_ni  ( rst_ni           ),
61:     .src_clk_i   ( clk_i            ),
62:     .src_data_i  ( core_dmi_resp_i  ),
63:     .src_valid_i ( core_dmi_valid_i ),
64:     .src_ready_o ( core_dmi_ready_o ),
65: 
66:     .dst_rst_ni  ( trst_ni          ),
67:     .dst_clk_i   ( tck_i            ),
68:     .dst_data_o  ( jtag_dmi_resp_o  ),
69:     .dst_valid_o ( jtag_dmi_valid_o ),
70:     .dst_ready_i ( jtag_dmi_ready_i )
71:   );
72: endmodule

{
  "scope_id": "piton_ariane_peripherals_debug_fuse_hmac_scope",
  "module2_inspection_results": [
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-JTAG-DMI-AUTH-001",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "external JTAG requester through tck_i/tms_i/td_i",
      "operation": "issue read/write DMI transactions and trigger debug actions",
      "object": "debug module DMI request path and debug capabilities: debug_req/debug_resp to dm_top, debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o",
      "expected_guard": "DMI requests reaching dm_top should be gated by persistent successful authentication/unlock state such as jtag_unlock/pass_check/hashValid, not only by TAP DMIACCESS/update_dr.",
      "observed_behavior": "The TAP can select DMIACCESS solely from the JTAG IR value. In dmi_jtag, an update_dr on dmi_access captures address/data and can transition to Read when `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)`. Since pass_check is combinationally defaulted to 0 and not a persistent state register, reads are allowed whenever we_flag is 0, without authenticated unlock. The generated dmi_req_valid is then passed through dmi_cdc directly to dm_top in riscv_peripherals. Writes require `pass_check == 1`, but pass_check appears non-persistent and only asserted transiently in PassChkValid.",
      "path": "JTAG pins -> riscv_peripherals.i_dmi_jtag -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS/update_dr -> dmi_jtag Read/Write -> dmi_cdc -> riscv_peripherals.debug_req/debug_req_valid -> dm_top DMI input",
      "state_condition": "On trst_ni low, dmi_jtag state resets to Idle and address/data/error clear. In Idle, pass_check is defaulted to 0. DMI read transition is permitted if `~we_flag == 1`, even with no pass_check. dmi_reset only clears error_q; dmi_rst_no is just rst_ni and is not connected at top.",
      "impact": "A locked external JTAG requester may issue DMI reads if we_flag_4 is deasserted, reaching the debug module without successful HMAC/password authorization. Depending on dm_top register semantics, this can expose or manipulate debug state.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "dmi_jtag.sv:103: `assign jtag_unlock_o = pass_check;`",
        "dmi_jtag.sv:114-116: dmi_req_valid/pass_check/pass_mode default to 0 in always_comb.",
        "dmi_jtag.sv:121-129: in Idle on `dmi_access && update_dr`, READ transitions when `(pass_check | ~we_flag == 1)`; WRITE requires `pass_check == 1`; PASS enters Write/pass_mode.",
        "dmi_jtag.sv:137-139 and 152-154: Read/Write states assert `dmi_req_valid = 1'b1`.",
        "dmi_jtag.sv:299-317: dmi_cdc connects local `dmi_req_valid` to output `dmi_req_valid_o` / `dmi_req_o`.",
        "dmi_cdc.sv:45-56: request CDC forwards `jtag_dmi_valid_i` to `core_dmi_valid_o`.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:275-289: i_dmi_jtag connects `dmi_req_o` to `debug_req` and `dmi_req_valid_o` to `debug_req_valid`.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:381-386: dm_top receives `dmi_req_valid_i(debug_req_valid)` and `dmi_req_i(debug_req)`."
      ]
    },
    {
      "obligation_id": "PERM-FUSE-HASH-READ-002",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "software/MMIO/NoC requester reaching fuse_mem via pkt_wrapper fuse_req/fuse_addr",
      "operation": "read/expose authentication hash or fuse words",
      "object": "jtag_hash / authentication fuse memory contents and other fuse-resident key material",
      "expected_guard": "Fuse/hash contents used for debug authentication should not be readable through a generic fuse read path without explicit privilege/access-control/lock mediation, and security-sensitive fuse words should be non-readable or separately protected.",
      "observed_behavior": "fuse_mem contains the JTAG expected HMAC hash in the same constant mem array used by rdata_o. rdata_o is assigned directly from mem[addr_q] for any requested address below MEM_SIZE, with no privilege, lock, or address filtering inside fuse_mem. Top-level connects pkt_wrapper fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata to fuse_mem while also continuously routing jtag_hash_o/ikey_hash_o/okey_hash_o to dmi_jtag. pkt_wrapper receives an acct_ctrl_i, but its internals are not in visible scope; fuse_mem itself does not prevent reading JTAG hash indices.",
      "path": "NoC pkt port -> noc_axilite_bridge -> pkt_wrapper -> fuse_req/fuse_addr -> fuse_mem.rdata_o; also fuse_mem.jtag_hash_o -> riscv_peripherals.jtag_hash -> dmi_jtag.jtag_hash_i",
      "state_condition": "fuse_mem has no reset; mem is constant. On any clk_i where req_i is asserted, addr_q captures addr_i low bits. rdata_o then reflects mem[addr_q] if addr_q < MEM_SIZE. JTAG hash is continuously assigned from mem[JTAG_OFFSET-1 : JTAG_OFFSET-8].",
      "impact": "If pkt_wrapper exposes fuse_req/fuse_addr to MMIO/NoC requesters without adequate filtering, the debug authentication hash and other fuse secrets/keys can be read, undermining JTAG authentication and other crypto protections.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "fuse_mem.sv:18-21: mem contains `// JTAG expected hamc hash` words.",
        "fuse_mem.sv:127-130: `if (req_i) addr_q <= addr_i[...]` with no privilege/lock check.",
        "fuse_mem.sv:135: `assign rdata_o = (addr_q < MEM_SIZE) ? mem[addr_q] : '0;`",
        "fuse_mem.sv:137-139: jtag_hash_o/okey_hash_o/ikey_hash_o are constructed directly from mem entries.",
        "riscv_peripherals.sv:1613-1615: declares fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata.",
